extra.reasoning:
The property is violated. Enum order low-to-high is self_care < routine < urgent < emergent. Rule r3 has the highest priority (100), requires palpitations=true, and sets acuity to the LOWEST value (self_care), so activating it by turning palpitations true can collapse a high acuity down to the bottom.

Baseline: chest_pain=true, dyspnea=true, palpitations=FALSE, sweating=false. Matching rules: r1 (urgent, prio 2) and r4 (emergent, prio 3); r3 does not match (palpitations false). Highest priority match is r4 -> acuity = emergent.

Flip palpitations to TRUE (all else unchanged): chest_pain=true, dyspnea=true, palpitations=TRUE, sweating=false. Matching rules: r1 (urgent, prio 2), r4 (emergent, prio 3), and now r3 (self_care, prio 100). Highest priority match is r3 -> acuity = self_care.

Setting palpitations true changes acuity from emergent (highest) to self_care (lowest), a strict decrease, violating the monotonicity property. The witness_assignment gives the violating 'after' input (palpitations=true); the comparison 'before' input is identical except palpitations=false.
